title: Earthy Japanese Tofu Ramen
description: This low-calorie ramen pairs tofu marinated in miso and buttermilk with fresh greens and mushrooms for an earthy, romantic bowl.
instructions: Whisk 2 tbsp of buttermilk with 2 tbsp miso paste in a bowl until smooth.
Slice firm tofu into 1/2-inch cubes and pat dry with paper towels.
Heat 1 tsp sesame oil in a pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add sliced tofu and sauté until lightly browned on all sides, about 3 minutes.
Pour the miso-buttermilk mixture over the tofu and stir to coat evenly.
Add 4 cups low-sodium vegetable broth, 1 tsp soy sauce, and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 8 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
While the broth simmers, cook ramen noodles according to package instructions in a separate pot.
Drain noodles and divide between two bowls, then ladle the hot miso tofu broth over them.
Garnish each bowl with sliced scallions, fresh spinach, and sliced shiitake mushrooms, and serve immediately.
